title: SA government on the possibility of Aussie-style social media ban
description: NATIONAL NEWS - The chance of the South...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"NATIONAL NEWS - The chance of the South African government considering a ban on social media for children is unlikely.","html":"NATIONAL NEWS - The chance of the South African government considering a ban on social media for children is unlikely."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Minister in The Presidency Khumbudzo Ntshavheni commented on the issue on Wednesday as she gave her final cabinet meeting briefing of the year.","html":"Minister in The Presidency Khumbudzo Ntshavheni commented on the issue on Wednesday as she gave her final cabinet meeting briefing of the year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When asked about following Australia’s example in banning under-16s from certain social media sites, Ntshavheni said South Africa needed to stick to its own path.","html":"When asked about following Australia’s example in banning under-16s from certain social media sites, Ntshavheni said South Africa needed to stick to its own path."},{"type":"heading","text":"Different developmental needs","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Australia’s ban on under-16s using social media began on Wednesday in the first international experiment in population-wide digital ID self-verification.","html":"Australia’s ban on under-16s using social media began on Wednesday in the first international experiment in population-wide digital ID self-verification."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Affected apps include Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, X and YouTube, with each platform implementing its own verification systems.","html":"Affected apps include Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, X and YouTube, with each platform implementing its own verification systems."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Among the multiple other cabinet meeting outcomes, Ntshavheni said that South Africa had just finalised its fifth National Plan of Action for Children (NPAC)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The minister added that South Africa and Australia are very different nations with different development needs and objectives.","html":"The minister added that South Africa and Australia are very different nations with different development needs and objectives."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We have just tabled our approach on protecting children. They are not even in the African continent,” she said.","html":"“We have just tabled our approach on protecting children. They are not even in the African continent,” she said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We have got the strategy to make sure that children are protected, that they are protected from malnutrition and that we support them to be active citizens.","html":"“We have got the strategy to make sure that children are protected, that they are protected from malnutrition and that we support them to be active citizens."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We protect their rights and their needs, we support their development and learning. Let’s focus on South Africa’s path and let’s engage on that path.”","html":"“We protect their rights and their needs, we support their development and learning. Let’s focus on South Africa’s path and let’s engage on that path.”"},{"type":"heading","text":"‘National strategy for acceleration’","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The NPAC is a five-year framework to be overseen by the department of social development.","html":"The NPAC is a five-year framework to be overseen by the department of social development."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“This is a national strategy for acceleration. This is a strategic framework to address the rights, needs and well-being of children in the country.","html":"“This is a national strategy for acceleration. This is a strategic framework to address the rights, needs and well-being of children in the country."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The plan aligns with the international frameworks, particularly the United Nations conventions on the rights of the child and national legislation,” Ntshavheni said.","html":"“The plan aligns with the international frameworks, particularly the United Nations conventions on the rights of the child and national legislation,” Ntshavheni said."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Other matters touched on by Ntshavheni included schools withholding reports from parents who had not paid school fees."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Public schools they do not withhold report cards. When you can not afford to pay school fees, you go and do the necessary applications,”","html":"“Public schools they do not withhold report cards. When you can not afford to pay school fees, you go and do the necessary applications,”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The ones whose report cards are being held are not in the public education system. South Africa provides a free, basic and quality education system for our children,” she said.","html":"“The ones whose report cards are being held are not in the public education system. South Africa provides a free, basic and quality education system for our children,” she said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen","html":" Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: SA government on the possibility of Aussie-style social media ban
